mitre_det_name: Email Forwarding Rule Abuse Detection Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Modification of Thunderbird message filters file or execution of CLI tools (e.g., formail/procmail) that alter .forward behavior.

mitre_det_name: Email Forwarding Rule Abuse Detection Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Creation of email forwarding/redirect rules in Exchange Online via New-InboxRule or transport rule cmdlets, including auto-forwarding address field usage.

mitre_det_name: Email Forwarding Rule Abuse Detection Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Creation or modification of Apple Mail rules by accessing plist files or GUI automation (AppleScript).

mitre_det_name: Email Forwarding Rule Abuse Detection Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Creation of inbox rules via PowerShell (New-InboxRule) or transport rules using Exchange cmdlets. Correlates user behavior, cmdlet usage, and rule properties.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection Strategy for Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
mitre_an_desc: Detects unusual outbound file transfer behavior using protocols like FTP, SMB, SMTP, or DNS, involving non-standard processes, off-hour activity, or uncommonly high volume.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection Strategy for Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
mitre_an_desc: Detects outbound traffic from hostd/vpxa or guest VM interfaces using unauthorized protocols such as FTP, HTTP POST bursts, or long-lived DNS tunnels.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection Strategy for Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
mitre_an_desc: Detects file exfiltration using tools like curl, scp, or custom binaries over protocols such as FTP, HTTP/S, or DNS tunneling, especially outside baseline user behavior.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection Strategy for Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
mitre_an_desc: Detects access to cloud APIs or CLI tools to move or sync files from sensitive buckets to external endpoints using protocols like HTTPS or S3 APIs.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection Strategy for Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
mitre_an_desc: Detects non-native file transfer via curl, Python scripts, or AppleScript using uncommon protocols like FTP, SMTP, or DNS exfiltration through mDNSResponder abuse.

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Exfiltration Over Web Service
mitre_an_desc: Abnormal API calls from user accounts invoking file upload endpoints outside normal baselines (M365, Google Drive, Box). Defender perspective: monitor unified audit logs for elevated frequency of Upload, Create, or Copy operations from compromised accounts.

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Exfiltration Over Web Service
mitre_an_desc: Processes that normally do not initiate network communications suddenly making outbound HTTPS connections with high outbound-to-inbound data ratios. Defender view: correlation between process creation logs (e.g., Word, Excel, PowerShell) and subsequent anomalous network traffic volumes toward common web services (Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive).

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Exfiltration Over Web Service
mitre_an_desc: Processes (tar, curl, python scripts) accessing large file sets and initiating outbound HTTPS POST requests with payload sizes inconsistent with baseline activity. Defender perspective: detect abnormal sequence of file archival followed by encrypted uploads to external web services.

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:EXECVE
mitre_log_sources.channel: curl or wget with POST/PUT options

mitre_log_sources.name: NSM:Flow
mitre_log_sources.channel: sustained outbound HTTPS sessions with high data volume

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Exfiltration Over Web Service
mitre_an_desc: Office apps or scripts writing files followed by xattr manipulation (to evade quarantine) and subsequent HTTPS uploads. Defender perspective: anomalous file modification + outbound TLS traffic originating from non-networking apps (Word, Excel, Preview).

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: execution of Office binaries with network activity

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: read/write of user documents prior to upload

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: outbound TLS connections to cloud storage providers

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Exfiltration Over Web Service
mitre_an_desc: ESXi guest OS or management interface processes establishing unexpected external HTTPS connections. Defender perspective: monitor vmx or hostd processes making outbound web requests with significant data transfer.

mitre_log_sources.name: esxi:vmkernel
mitre_log_sources.channel: network session initiation with external HTTPS services

mitre_log_sources.name: esxi:hostd
mitre_log_sources.channel: file copy or datastore upload via HTTPS

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Command and Scripting Interpreter Abuse
mitre_an_desc: Detects use of shell interpreters (e.g., bash, sh, python, perl) initiated by users or processes not normally executing them, especially when chaining suspicious utilities like netcat, curl, or ssh.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Command and Scripting Interpreter Abuse
mitre_an_desc: Detects launch of command-line interpreters via Terminal, Automator, or hidden `osascript`, especially when parent process lineage deviates from user-initiated applications.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Command and Scripting Interpreter Abuse
mitre_an_desc: Detects use of 'esxcli system' or direct interpreter commands (e.g., busybox shell) invoked from SSH or host terminal unexpectedly.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Command and Scripting Interpreter Abuse
mitre_an_desc: Identifies CLI interpreter access (e.g., Cisco IOS, Juniper JUNOS) via `enable` mode or scripting-capable sessions used by uncommon accounts or from unknown IPs.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Command and Scripting Interpreter Abuse
mitre_an_desc: Detects the execution of scripting or command interpreters (e.g., powershell.exe, cmd.exe, wscript.exe) outside expected administrative time windows or from abnormal user contexts, often followed by encoded/obfuscated arguments or secondary execution events.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Exfiltration Over Unencrypted Non-C2 Protocol
mitre_an_desc: Detects use of unencrypted protocols (e.g., TFTP, FTP, HTTP) to transfer configuration files, routing tables, or logs to untrusted IP addresses, especially using administrative commands like `copy run ftp:`.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Exfiltration Over Unencrypted Non-C2 Protocol
mitre_an_desc: Detects shell-based scripts accessing configuration files or snapshots and transmitting them over unencrypted protocols such as FTP or HTTP to non-management IPs.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Exfiltration Over Unencrypted Non-C2 Protocol
mitre_an_desc: Detects file access or compression utilities followed by outbound connections using curl, wget, ftp, or custom binaries communicating over unencrypted protocols.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Exfiltration Over Unencrypted Non-C2 Protocol
mitre_an_desc: Detects abnormal outbound HTTP/FTP connections by local scripts or binaries outside of standard browser activity, following access to local documents or user data.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Exfiltration Over Unencrypted Non-C2 Protocol
mitre_an_desc: Detects data access or staging events followed by outbound data flows using unencrypted protocols (e.g., FTP, HTTP) initiated by unexpected processes or to rare destinations.

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
mitre_an_desc: Processes such as curl, wget, rclone, or custom scripts executing uploads to cloud storage endpoints. Defender perspective: detect chained events where tar/gzip is executed to compress files followed by HTTPS PUT/POST requests to known storage services.

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
mitre_an_desc: Applications or scripts invoking cloud storage APIs (Dropbox sync, iCloud, Google Drive client) in unexpected contexts. Defender perspective: detect sensitive file reads by non-standard applications followed by unusual encrypted uploads to external cloud storage domains.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: execution of curl, rclone, or Office apps invoking network sessions

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: outbound HTTPS connections to cloud storage APIs

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
mitre_an_desc: Unusual ESXi processes (vmx, hostd) reading datastore files and generating outbound HTTPS traffic toward external cloud storage endpoints. Defender perspective: anomalous datastore activity followed by network transfers to Dropbox, AWS S3, or other storage services.

mitre_log_sources.name: esxi:vmkernel
mitre_log_sources.channel: network flows to external cloud services

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
mitre_an_desc: Unusual processes (e.g., powershell.exe, excel.exe) accessing large local files and subsequently initiating HTTPS POST requests to domains associated with cloud storage services (e.g., dropbox.com, drive.google.com, box.com). Defender perspective: correlation between file reads in sensitive directories and high outbound traffic volume to known storage APIs.

mitre_det_name: Detect Local Email Collection via Outlook Data File Access and Command Line Tooling
mitre_an_desc: Detection focuses on processes that attempt to locate, access, or exfiltrate local Outlook data files (.pst/.ost) using file system access, native Windows utilities (e.g., PowerShell, WMI), or remote access tools with file browsing capabilities. The behavior chain includes directory enumeration, file access, optional compression or staging, and network transfer.

mitre_det_name: Detect Remote Email Collection via Abnormal Login and Programmatic Access
mitre_an_desc: Monitors programmatic access to user mailboxes in cloud-based email systems (e.g., O365, Exchange Online) using APIs or tokens. Focuses on OAuth misuse, suspicious MailItemsAccessed patterns, scripted keyword searches, and connections from untrusted agents or locations.

mitre_log_sources.name: azure:signinlogs
mitre_log_sources.channel: Suspicious login to cloud mailbox system

mitre_det_name: Detect Remote Email Collection via Abnormal Login and Programmatic Access
mitre_an_desc: Detects adversaries accessing remote mail systems (e.g., Exchange Online, O365) using stolen credentials or OAuth tokens, followed by scripted access to mailbox contents via PowerShell, AADInternals, or unattended API queries. Detection focuses on abnormal logon sessions, user agents, IP locations, and scripted or tool-based email data access.

mitre_log_sources.name: azure:signinlogs
mitre_log_sources.channel: Abnormal sign-in from scripting tools (PowerShell, AADInternals)

mitre_det_name: Detection of Data Staging Prior to Exfiltration
mitre_an_desc: Detects files collected into user temp or shared directories followed by compression with ditto, zip, or custom scripts.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Data Staging Prior to Exfiltration
mitre_an_desc: Detects staging of sensitive files into temporary or public directories, compression with 7zip/WinRAR, or batch copy prior to exfiltration.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Data Staging Prior to Exfiltration
mitre_an_desc: Detects snapshots or data stored in VMFS volumes from root CLI or remote agents.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Data Staging Prior to Exfiltration
mitre_an_desc: Detects virtual disk expansion or file copy operations to cloud buckets or mounted volumes from isolated instances.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Data Staging Prior to Exfiltration
mitre_an_desc: Detects script or user activity copying files to a central temp or /mnt directory followed by archive/compression utilities.
